students.name: Alex
students.studentUnderstandings: Alex demonstrated a solid understanding of adding fractions with unlike denominators today. They correctly identified the meaning of a denominator and numerator during the warm-up. Throughout the session, Alex accurately converted fractions, found common denominators, and explained their reasoning, such as converting one half to three sixths. Alex also capably solved the real-world pizza word problem and contributed to the final challenge problem.

students.name: Jordan
students.studentUnderstandings: Jordan showed strong comprehension of fraction addition today. They correctly explained the process of finding a common denominator at the start and accurately converted fractions throughout the session, such as one third to two sixths. Jordan correctly defined improper fractions and explained how to convert them to mixed numbers. They also successfully found the least common multiple for six and eight during the challenge problem.

students.name: Morgan
students.studentUnderstandings: Morgan did excellent work with adding fractions today. They accurately defined the numerator and denominator during the warm-up. Morgan completed several fraction addition problems, correctly finding common denominators and converting improper fractions to mixed numbers, such as converting twenty-three twentieths. They also identified that seventeen is prime and cannot be simplified in the final challenge, showing deeper number sense.

internalFeedback.lessonTitle: Cignition: Adding Fractions with Unlike Denominators
